summary: Advanced matching for the Ice Mountain stage file `gricemt`, with struct/layout corrections, real implementations for several callbacks and helpers, and a symbol-boundary fix around `fn_801F9150`/`fn_801F91A4`. The decomp.dev report shows +3844 matched bytes, +0.10% matched code, 13 new matches, and 11 additional improvements in `main/melee/gr/gricemt`.

key_files.role: Main Ice Mountain decomp work: data typing, callback implementations, segment movement helpers, assertions, stack padding, and field-use corrections.
Adjusted `grIceMt_GroundVars` and `grIceMt_GroundVars2` layout, padding, and ownership of fields such as `xF8`, `x100`, and `x108`.
Updated Ice Mountain prototypes and params, including `IceMountainParams.xB0/xB4` as `s16*`, `fn_801F8E58`, `fn_801F9150`, `grIceMt_801F96E0`, `grIceMt_801FA0BC`, and `grIceMt_801FA500` signatures.
Corrected function boundary by extending `fn_801F9150` from size `0x54` to `0x58` and removing separate `fn_801F91A4`.

what_changed: Replaced placeholder or partial code in `gricemt.c` with matching implementations for Ice Mountain callbacks/helpers including `fn_801F8E58`, `fn_801F9150`, `fn_801F9338`, `fn_801F9448`, `fn_801F9558`, `grIceMt_801F8B10`, `grIceMt_801F993C`, `grIceMt_801F9ACC`, `grIceMt_801FA0BC`, `grIceMt_801FA500`, and `grIceMt_801FA7F0`.
Added typed stage data: `grIm_803B8220` became `static const Vec3`, `IceMtRowData.x4/x8` became `f32`, and `grIm_803E4068` was initialized with six row records such as `{ 1, 180.0f, -180.0f }`.
Introduced small local/extern rodata helper types `GrIm588` and `GrIm825C` to copy packed joint-index data into stack temporaries before calls to `grIceMt_801F8CDC`.

Reworked many Ice Mountain ground-var accesses from incorrect `corneria` or `icemt` members to `gv.icemt`/`gv.icemt2`, especially moving material object arrays to `gp->gv.icemt2.xF8` and using `gp->gv.icemt.xC4/xC6` as segment IDs.

Implemented segment-distance and segment-scroll logic: `grIceMt_801F993C` computes spacing from `grIm_803E4068` and `grIm_804D69F4->x40`; `grIceMt_801F9ACC` rotates active segment IDs, creates/destroys ground objects, updates translate-Y, and refreshes collision/JObj pointers.
Implemented `grIceMt_801FA0BC` to initialize/reposition a pair of Ice Mountain segments, set active flags, and refresh collision pointers before calling `grIceMt_801FA854`.

Updated callback setup functions such as `grIceMt_801F7D94`, `grIceMt_801F7F70`, `grIceMt_801F8208`, `grIceMt_801F83EC`, `grIceMt_801F865C`, and `grIceMt_801F8850` to seed `icemt2` flags and call `grIceMt_801F8CDC` with the right joint-index arrays.

Implemented collision callbacks `fn_801F9338`, `fn_801F9448`, and `fn_801F9558` to check `CollData.x34_flags.b1234`, set per-segment state arrays, run `grAnime_801C7A04`, `grAnime_801C7B24`, and `grAnime_801C78FC`, then delegate to `grIceMt_801FA7F0`.
Converted explicit `__assert("gricemt.c", line, expr)` blocks to `HSD_ASSERT`/`HSD_ASSERTMSG` with matching source line numbers, requiring the new `baselib/debug.h` include.

Changed `fn_801F9150` from returning `IceMountainParams*` to returning an `s32` segment value with an out parameter, reading `grIm_804D69F4->xB0/xB4`; removed the separate empty `fn_801F91A4` symbol/declaration.
Changed `grIceMt_801FA500` from a one-argument placeholder to a two-argument traversal over a JObj tree that returns the index of a target `HSD_JObj` or `-1`.
Updated `grIceMt_801F8B10` from placeholder math to `f32` translate-Y wraparound using `0.3f * gp->gv.icemt2.xC4` and `3500.0f * Ground_801C0498()`.
Updated `grIceMt_801F8CDC` material setup to use literal `0.0f`, `-1.0f`, `1.0f`, and `6.0f` instead of fake float globals, and asserted `count <= 20`, `parent_jobj`, and `child_jobj`.
Adjusted `types.h` layout comments and padding for `grIceMt_GroundVars` and expanded `grIceMt_GroundVars2` with `HSD_GObj* xF8[20]`.
The automated report recorded 13 new 100% matches and 11 unmatched-item improvements, while listing `fn_801F91A4` as a broken match because the diff removed that function boundary.
smart_moves: The function-boundary correction in `symbols.txt` is important: expanding `fn_801F9150` to `0x58` and deleting `fn_801F91A4` aligns the symbol map with the implemented control flow instead of preserving a fake 4-byte stub.

Separating `icemt` state from `icemt2` state fixed many suspicious union accesses; moving `xF8` material GObj storage to `grIceMt_GroundVars2` made the create/destroy loops and header layout agree with observed use.

Using small stack structs (`GrIm588`, `GrIm825C`, anonymous `{ u32 x0; u32 x4; }`) to copy rodata before passing as `s16*` matched packed joint-index data without pretending it was a higher-level named asset.
Replacing fake global float constants (`grIm_804DB574`, `grIm_804DB5B0`, `grIm_804DB5B4`, `grIm_804DB5B8`) with literals reduced bad sdata2 modeling and made material-setup calls clearer.
Line-number-preserving `HSD_ASSERT`/`HSD_ASSERTMSG` calls kept debug checks compact while preserving matching-sensitive assert metadata.

The PR reused existing engine helpers instead of open-coding behavior: `Ground_801C2BA4`, `Ground_801C4A08`, `Ground_801C3214`, `Ground_801C32AC`, `Ground_801C2FE0`, `Ground_801C2D0C`, `Ground_801C3FA4`, and `grMaterial_801C8CDC/8CFC/8DE0`.
Adding tiny inline wrappers around `grIceMt_801F71E8` appears to be a matching tactic for call shape/inlining in `grIceMt_801F9ACC` and `grIceMt_801FA0BC` without changing the helper’s semantics.
The code keeps unknown field names (`xC4`, `xC6`, `x100`, `x108`) but improves their type and ownership, which is safer than over-renaming based on weak evidence.
decomp_lessons: When a 4-byte function appears immediately after a larger function, verify boundaries against real control flow; it may be an artifact to merge into the preceding function, as with `fn_801F9150` and removed `fn_801F91A4`.
For stage `Ground` union fields, incorrect reuse of another stage’s variant (`corneria`) can block matching and obscure real layout; switch to the correct variant before refining helper signatures.
Do not model all rodata/sdata as float globals. If call sites need packed `s16` or `u32` data copied onto the stack, introduce small temporary structs that match the observed load/store pattern.

For stage segment systems, helper signatures may need to accept the state substruct directly (`struct grIceMt_GroundVars*`) rather than a generic `HSD_GObj*` when the function operates on segment ID fields.
Explicit padding in `types.h` can be more valuable than guessed semantic names when the field layout is still unknown but offset-sensitive.
Use `f32` literals (`0.3f`, `0.5f`, `3500.0f`, `0.0f`) when the target uses single-precision math; accidental doubles were removed from several placeholders.
Automated match reports should be read with context: a listed broken match can be the expected result of a corrected symbol split/merge rather than a regression.
For JObj traversal helpers like `grIceMt_801FA500`, matching may depend on exact traversal order using child/next/parent links and flag checks such as `jobj->flags & 0x1000`.

Stage collision callbacks often both trigger animation state and delegate to a shared stage-interaction handler; here `fn_801F9338`, `fn_801F9448`, and `fn_801F9558` all end by calling `grIceMt_801FA7F0`.
naming_conventions: Ice Mountain globals use `grIm_` prefixes with addresses or data offsets, while functions use `grIceMt_` or anonymous `fn_801F....` names until semantics are known.

Keep assertion-expression variable names meaningful and matching-aware: the diff uses `bg_gobj`, `bg_gp`, `mgobj`, `jobj`, `parent_jobj`, and `child_jobj`, matching original assert strings such as `"bg_gobj"` and `"mgobj"`.
Unknown struct fields remain offset names (`xC4`, `xC6`, `xD8`, `x100`, `x108`, `xF8`) but were moved to the correct `icemt`/`icemt2` structs and annotated with clearer offset comments.
Temporary rodata structs are named after addresses/offsets (`GrIm588`, `GrIm825C`) rather than guessed gameplay names, which is appropriate when only packing/layout is proven.

Callback types are made explicit at call sites with casts such as `(HSD_GObjEvent) fn_801F8E58` and `(mpLib_Callback) fn_801F9338` when the target signature is known but generic callback APIs are used.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(0x10)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, and `PAD_STACK(4)` in multiple functions to match stack frame size/alignment.
Converted manual assert blocks to `HSD_ASSERT(line, expr)` and `HSD_ASSERTMSG(line, expr, msg)` to preserve source-line constants while simplifying C structure.
Introduced stack temporaries populated from rodata (`sp14 = grIm_804DB588`, `sp14.x0 = grIm_804DB590`, `sp14.hi = grIm_803B825C.hi`) before passing them as `s16*`, matching expected memory layout.
Used `static inline` wrapper chains (`grIceMt_801F71E8_inner/noinline` and `_inner2/noinline2`) around `grIceMt_801F71E8` to tune generated call/inlining shape.
Replaced hard-coded segment IDs in helpers with reads from `gp->gv.icemt.xC4/xC6`, which both clarified semantics and improved matching for moving segment updates.
Used exact single-precision math and wraparound comparisons in `grIceMt_801F8B10` and spacing math in `grIceMt_801F993C`.
Adjusted symbol sizes in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` alongside C/header changes so decomp.dev compares the intended function boundaries.
Preserved line-number constants in assertions such as `0x27C`, `0x475`, `0x7D4`, `0xA9D`, `0xAB9`, and `0xBB1`, which are often matching-sensitive in this codebase.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice; the PR body was empty.
decomp.dev reported `Matched code: 69.06% (+0.10%, +3844 bytes)` and total functions `19829 (-1)`.

decomp.dev reported 13 new matches in `main/melee/gr/gricemt`: `fn_801F9338`, `fn_801F9448`, `fn_801F9558`, `grIceMt_801F7F70`, `grIceMt_801F83EC`, `grIceMt_801F8B10`, `grIceMt_801F796C`, `fn_801F9150`, `grIceMt_801F865C`, `grIceMt_801FA7F0`, `grIceMt_801F8208`, `grIceMt_801F8850`, and `grIceMt_801F7D94`.

decomp.dev reported 11 improvements in unmatched items, led by `grIceMt_801F9ACC` (+1178 bytes, 18.88% to 96.34%), `grIceMt_801FA0BC` (+630 bytes, 0.00% to 92.78%), `fn_801F8E58` (+427 bytes, 0.00% to 89.13%), and `grIceMt_801F993C` (+399 bytes, 0.00% to 99.96%).
decomp.dev listed `fn_801F91A4` as one broken match, consistent with the diff removing that separate 4-byte symbol/declaration and folding the size into `fn_801F9150`.
follow_up_queries: Investigate remaining mismatch in `grIceMt_801F9ACC` after this PR: bot says 96.34%, not 100%.
Investigate remaining mismatch in `grIceMt_801FA0BC`: bot says 92.78%.
Investigate `grIceMt_801FA500` JObj traversal details: bot says 84.80%.
Confirm whether the removed `fn_801F91A4` boundary should stay deleted in all symbol maps and derived documentation.
Rename `grIceMt_GroundVars` fields `xC4`, `xC6`, `x100`, and `x108` once segment-pair and callback state semantics are better understood.
Audit other Ice Mountain callbacks for incorrect `icemt` versus `icemt2` field ownership, especially material arrays and per-segment flags.
Look for more fake `.sdata2` float globals in stage files that should be literals or packed rodata structs instead.
